chart_A_analysis: Chart A uses a stacked area chart to show global sales by genre over time. It clearly conveys overall market growth and decline and makes it easy to see which genres contribute most in particular years, highlighting dominance and shifts. However, the large number of genres makes it somewhat crowded, and precise comparison of individual genres across years is harder.
chart_A_readability_rationale: The time trends and dominant genres are visible at a glance, though overlapping areas and many colors slightly reduce clarity.
chart_A_precision_rationale: It effectively answers whether certain genres dominate in specific years, but exact values and fine-grained comparisons are difficult to read.
chart_A_appeal_rationale: The stacked areas and color palette are visually engaging and communicate a sense of market evolution, though the chart feels busy.
chart_B_analysis: Chart B presents the same data as a heatmap, with color intensity representing sales by genre and year. This makes it very easy to spot peaks, dominance, and shifts in genre popularity over time. Exact totals are less obvious, but relative dominance is very clear.
chart_B_readability_rationale: The heatmap allows quick scanning for dominant genres and changes over time with minimal visual clutter.
chart_B_precision_rationale: It directly answers the question by clearly showing which genres dominate in which years and how this changes, even if numerical precision is approximate.
chart_B_appeal_rationale: The color gradient is clean and intuitive, though it is more analytical than decorative.
preference_rationale: Chart B more clearly and directly highlights genre dominance and shifts over time, making it better suited to answering the question efficiently and accurately.
